meta.title: Community Leadership & Storytelling | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Community storytelling, advocacy, and leadership projects with clear separation between creative work and direct support.

hero.title: Community Leadership & Storytelling
hero.subtitle: Creative advocacy and storytelling that helps people show up, speak up, and be seen — with direct support arranged separately when needed.
hero.bestFor: People who want to tell their story or create a community-facing project.

blocks.html: This pathway helps people express their voice in community settings through creative advocacy, public storytelling, presentation practice, and participant-owned media projects.

When a project requires transportation, supervision, community accompaniment, or other direct support, those services must be arranged through the appropriate support lane rather than bundled into a creative-service scope.

items: Helping participants tell their story in their own way through video, audio, writing, art, or presentation
Creating positive digital content with the right consent and review process
Practicing how to describe preferences, goals, and support needs
Preparing a short presentation or introduction
Creating a project around a community partner, event, or cause
Reflecting on what the participant wants to try next
Identifying community partners such as coaches, artists, business owners, or volunteers
Finding places and people the participant wants to return to
More comfort sharing ideas in community settings
Creative materials that reflect the person's interests and goals

sessionStyle.title: Session Style
items: Direct support arranged separately when needed

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.prompt: Want to plan a community story or advocacy project?

blocks.html: Projects may include story planning, interviews, a short presentation, a community partner feature, or a media piece. Outings and supervision should be scoped and funded separately when required.
